scope: OpenCode-specific mapping and conformance evidence. All values in this artifact belong to the adapter or measured compatibility entry, never to the provider-agnostic engine.

rules: server_bind must resolve to loopback for this adapter profile
port and password are generated per run
session store uses a per-run adapter-owned locator when the compatibility entry proves isolation
durations are references to resolved RunSpec configuration, never constants in adapter code
provider/model/variant are passed from RunSpec and never chosen by the adapter

authority_boundary.runtime_owns: Deterministic state, dispatch ordering, cancellation authority, process ownership of the dedicated OpenCode server tree (startup, shutdown, supervision), evidence journal (append-only, intent-first). Runtime never assumes failure/success on ambiguous provider evidence; missing or conflicting evidence escalates to investigator with a visible blocked state.
authority_boundary.gateway_owns: Translation between runtime intents and OpenCode v1 HTTP+SSE protocol, server lifecycle operations, deduplication, cursor management (gateway-assigned monotonic sequence), signal reconciliation via REST, conformance checks, normalized metadata extraction, typed receipts compatible with DEC-026.
authority_boundary.opencode_owns: Provider authentication, model routing, tool permission enforcement, local message storage, SSE event emission.
authority_boundary.privacy_boundary: Runtime journal stores only observation metadata (identifiers, type, byte count, digest, locator). Raw stream prompt, reasoning, text deltas, tool content, and provider payloads do not enter that journal. OpenCode local storage retains raw messages. The declared role output MUST be validated and materialized as a durable shared-protocol artifact because downstream work depends on it. Point 5 will define redaction, access, retention, and deletion without making output durability optional.
authority_boundary.security_boundary: One dedicated supervised OpenCode server and one isolated OPENCODE_DB file per run/config trust boundary. Ephemeral loopback port and Basic password are generated per run. Provider credentials remain in OpenCode's normal auth store and are not copied. Password and DB path are excluded from prompts, artifacts, logs, and journal payloads, but same-run child processes may inherit or discover them. This contains cross-run session accidents only while the pinned adapter proves DB isolation; it is not same-user adversarial isolation.
authority_boundary.process_supervisor: Runtime owns the dedicated server process tree. Gateway exposes startup/shutdown operations with ProcessSupervisorReceipt.

write_authority.source_of_truth_writer: Only the fenced runtime controller appends to the DEC-026 journal and allocates run_sequence. AgentGateway never opens the SQLite source of truth directly.
write_authority.gateway_output: Gateway returns typed receipt proposals and normalized observations with gateway_sequence and SourceObservationKey. The controller validates them, assigns transactional run_sequence, and appends them or rejects them.
write_authority.gateway_sequence_scope: Monotonic per run and server_generation for observation reconstruction only. It is not authority ordering and cannot replace run_sequence.
write_authority.concurrency: Controller append transaction enforces unique operation_key and SourceObservationKey. Concurrent duplicate receipt proposals resolve to one appended fact; stale controller generations are fenced by DEC-026.

operation_authorization.validation: Before spawn, create, prompt, abort, force termination, or output materialization, AgentGateway verifies that the supplied intent receipt was committed by the active fenced controller generation and matches the requested operation. Read-only probes append observations afterward and cannot produce terminal authority by themselves.
operation_authorization.stale_call: A missing, stale, mismatched, or already-finalized intent receipt rejects the external call before it reaches OpenCode or the process supervisor.

health_check.description: Verify OpenCode server liveness and version compatibility before any session or lifecycle operation.

outputs.server_generation: string (controller generation plus ProcessSupervisorReceipt identity; never timestamp or PID alone)

preconditions: Server process is running on known port with password set.
postconditions: Gateway records server_version and server_generation. Unhealthy or version not in allowlist rejects further operations.

verify_compatibility.description: Validate the exact backend binary and API surface against a previously completed conformance run before dispatch.
preconditions: OpenAPI is fetched from the authenticated loopback server.
CLI wrapper version is recorded for diagnostics but is never substituted for server_health_version.
postconditions: The tuple exactly matches an enabled compatibility-matrix entry whose full conformance suite passed.
Any binary, server version, OpenAPI digest, adapter version, or platform change invalidates the receipt and rejects dispatch until conformance is rerun.

verify_effective_profile.description: Confirm the active agent profile projection matches runtime intent before creating or resuming a session. Uses an allowlisted projection: requested agent, explicit model/provider/variant, ordered permissions (last-match-wins), allowed tools, role prompt digest, steps, directory, adapter config digest, and compatible server version receipt.

expected_permissions_ordered: string (ordered list of permission rules as evaluated last-match-wins)

preconditions: Server healthy. OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR alignment is verified.
postconditions: Mismatch (including silent fallback, version not allowlisted, or missing tool/permission) rejects dispatch; gateway does not create/resume session with unverified profile.

create_session.description: Create a new OpenCode session with explicit agent and model. OpenCode creation is not assumed idempotent; runtime metadata makes an ambiguous result reconcilable.
preconditions: Health check and profile verification passed.
SessionCreateIntent with session_operation_key, run_id, and config_digest is committed before POST /session.
No session with matching runtime metadata already exists on the dedicated server.
postconditions: POST /session includes metadata {run_id, session_operation_key, config_digest}.
SessionCreateReceipt is committed only after GET /session/:id confirms matching directory, agent, model, and metadata.
After an ambiguous POST result, recovery lists sessions and matches exact runtime metadata; it never blindly repeats creation. Zero matches remains blocked; more than one match is a policy violation.

server_startup.description: Start a dedicated supervised OpenCode server instance on an ephemeral loopback port with a generated Basic auth password. Returns a ProcessSupervisorReceipt.

inputs.session_db_path: absolute runtime-owned path for this run's OPENCODE_DB outside the worktree

receipt.password_hash: string (SHA-256 of password; secret not stored in journal)
receipt.server_generation: string (controller generation plus supervisor receipt identity)

preconditions: No conflicting server on the same config trust boundary. Port available on loopback.
postconditions: ServerStartIntent is committed before spawn.
The DEC-026 process supervisor establishes recoverable ownership or crash-close semantics and commits ProcessSupervisorReceipt before the child resumes.
The child receives an absolute per-run OPENCODE_DB path. Before session creation, GET /session proves the isolated store contains zero sessions; any existing or shared-session view rejects dispatch.
The isolated server still proves access to the explicitly selected already-authenticated provider without copying auth.json or credential values.
OpenCode binds only to 127.0.0.1, becomes healthy, and passes compatibility checks.

Gateway records ProcessSupervisorReceipt without the clear password. Password is excluded from prompts, artifacts, logs, and journal; same-run child inheritance remains an explicit security-level limitation.

server_shutdown.description: Terminate the dedicated OpenCode server process tree. Attempt graceful shutdown first, then forceful kill. Returns termination receipt.
preconditions: Server generation known from startup receipt.
postconditions: No live server process with that generation remains. Runtime records termination receipt.

prompt_async.description: Dispatch a prompt to an existing session. Gateway generates its own stable prompt_operation_key; OpenCode's messageID is correlation only. Gateway must persist intent before calling prompt_async. A 204 is admission acknowledgement only; delivery is confirmed by matching user message in GET /session/:id/message.

inputs.content_digest: string (hash of normalized prompt text plus settings)

outputs.request_acknowledged: boolean (true if 204 received; says nothing about durable delivery)

preconditions: Session exists and is not in a terminal error state.
Gateway has not already persisted a delivery for the same prompt_operation_key (duplicate detection).
Profile is still verified (re-verify if session was resumed after restart).
postconditions: The fenced runtime controller commits PromptIntent {prompt_operation_key, session_id, content_digest, status='intended'} and passes its receipt BEFORE AgentGateway calls prompt_async.
After 204, status becomes request_acknowledged. Gateway polls GET /session/:id/message for a user message matching both message_id and exact content_digest. Only then is status delivered.
If matching user message not found within configurable timeout, status set to 'ambiguous' and prompt blocked; no automatic resend. Blocked state escalated to investigator.

Only response classes proven by the versioned conformance matrix to occur before any effect may create negative_proof. Generic 4xx, connection reset, timeout, server restart, or message absence is ambiguous and never authorizes automatic resend.
prompt_async.deduplication_note: Gateway-level deduplication by prompt_operation_key. OpenCode messageID is NOT idempotent; sending same messageID twice concatenates text and triggers duplicate assistant response. Gateway must never rely on messageID for idempotency.

observe_events.description: Connect to the SSE bus, buffer events, deduplicate by evt_id (within same connection), normalize according to taxonomy, and classify as progress vs heartbeat. Cursor is gateway-assigned monotonic sequence, not evt_id. SSE is non-replayable; on disconnect, run REST reconciliation.

inputs.last_gateway_sequence: int (gateway-assigned last processed sequence number, 0 for fresh)

preconditions: Session exists and SSE endpoint is reachable.
Current BackendStartReceipt and CompatibilityReceipt provide adapter_version and server_generation used in every SourceObservationKey.
postconditions: New normalized events appended to gateway event buffer with gateway sequence, source observation key, and classification.
observe_events.normalization_rules: See event_taxonomy. Source observation key is SHA-256(adapter_version, server_generation, session_id, evt_id).
observe_events.cursor_semantics: Gateway sequence is incremental per event processed. On reconnect, gateway does NOT use Last-Event-Id header for replay; instead runs full REST reconciliation (see reconcile operation) to rebuild state.

reconcile.description: After SSE disconnect or controller restart, reconcile session state by cross-referencing REST signals. No SSE evt_id gap detection is used; all state is rebuilt from durable sources.
preconditions: Server health is verified.
postconditions: Gateway updates its view of session state based on reconciliation matrix. If state cannot be resolved, session is marked blocked and escalated to investigator.
signals_used: Runtime process tree (dedicated server tree termination status)

collect_output.description: Extract assistant response output and materialize declared role output as a validated shared-protocol envelope and ArtifactRef. Raw reasoning and telemetry stay external.
preconditions: Assistant message exists and is complete (finish_reason present, not in error unless abort).
postconditions: Gateway reads the declared final output, validates it against the shared role-protocol envelope, materializes its bytes in the runtime artifact store, verifies the digest, and appends ArtifactReceipt.
The journal stores ArtifactRef and metadata, not raw telemetry or reasoning. OpenCode local storage is evidence and recovery input, not the durable location of the promoted role output.

cancel.description: Initiate cancellation of a session's active prompt. Multi-stage: request abort (optional best-effort), verify server tree termination, and record cancelled effect. Provider abort acknowledgement is not required terminal evidence.
preconditions: Session exists. Runtime has recorded cancel intent.
postconditions: Gateway may POST /session/:id/abort (optional best-effort) and records acknowledge.
Gateway requests OpenCode abort, then waits RunSpec.cancellation_grace.
After grace, runtime force-terminates and verifies only the owned server tree if it is still alive.
Terminal condition: cancel intent recorded AND server tree confirmed terminated (or no process launched) AND cancel effect persisted in journal.
If tree does not terminate within grace, session enters blocked state and escalates to investigator.
OpenCode MessageAbortedError is provider evidence, not the root of authority; its presence or absence does not alone determine terminal state.

resume_session.description: Resume an existing session after gateway/runtime restart. Re-verify profile, reconcile state via REST, and ensure any incomplete prompt is resolved (cancelled/failed/blocked) before any new prompt is dispatched. Never silently continue an interrupted generation.
preconditions: Session ID and per-run DB locator are known from prior persisted facts.
A current CompatibilityReceipt matches the running binary digest, server health version, OpenAPI digest, adapter version, and platform.
verify_effective_profile passed against the committed RunSpec before any resume action.
postconditions: Profile re-verified; any change or drift rejects resume.
If only the controller restarted and the same supervised server_generation remains alive, SSE reconnects and the active turn may continue under reconciliation.
If the OpenCode server_generation restarted, an active provider turn is never assumed to continue; state is reconciled via REST and remains blocked unless typed terminal evidence exists.

Any prompt_operation_key with status intended, request_acknowledged, or ambiguous is resolved: exact message_id plus content_digest proves delivered; conformance-approved pre-effect evidence proves negative; every other outcome remains blocked. Old prompts are never silently continued or resent.
If the previous turn was interrupted, it may become cancelled or failed only with the typed evidence required by DEC-026; otherwise it remains blocked and no new prompt is accepted.
Only after prior turn fully resolved can a new prompt be dispatched.

stable_routes_used.GET /config (projected): canonical allowlisted effective-profile projection only; never digest entire response or store secrets
stable_routes_used.GET /provider (projected): provider facts relevant to explicit pinning only
stable_routes_used.GET /session: list all sessions in the selected directory; used to prove the per-run OPENCODE_DB is empty and to reconcile session creation by runtime metadata

routes_avoided.all /api/* v2 routes: Experimental; measured as non-functional for agent run. Advertised in OpenAPI but rejected by conformance tests. Must not be used in v1.

api_mapping.permission_semantics: Configuration sources merge; later sources override conflicts. Permission rules are ordered and the last matching rule wins. Effective permission must be evaluated with last-match-wins semantics, not by searching for any allow entry.
api_mapping.authentication: OpenCode loopback server Basic auth using OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD generated per run. Provider authentication stays in OpenCode's existing auth store. Session data uses a separate per-run OPENCODE_DB file; no credential file is copied. Clear secrets are excluded from prompts, artifacts, logs, and journal payloads.

raw_event_mapping.session.status: Emit session.active only on a material transition to busy; emit tool.retry/provider.retry on a new retry attempt. Unchanged polls are heartbeat only.
raw_event_mapping.session.idle: Emit session.idle transition; reconcile messages before any terminal verdict.
raw_event_mapping.message.updated: Use stable message ID, role, finish, error, token/cost metadata, and structural digest to emit assistant.message.created or turn terminal events. Raw content is not journaled.
raw_event_mapping.message.part.updated: Map part type and state to reasoning/text/tool lifecycle; emit only on new ID, state, size, or digest.
raw_event_mapping.message.part.delta: Map reasoning/text/tool field deltas to byte-count and rolling-digest changes; raw delta remains outside the journal.
raw_event_mapping.session.diff: Emit artifact.updated only when normalized file/diff digest changes.
raw_event_mapping.session.error: Record provider error observation and reconcile; it is not runtime FailureEvidence until verified.
raw_event_mapping.permission.asked|permission.v2.asked|que: Emit one blocked protocol event and apply noninteractive policy; repeated waiting does not reset progress.
raw_event_mapping.unknown_event: Record type, event ID, size, and digest as unsupported observation, run reconciliation, and do not count it as progress or terminal evidence.

session.active.description: Material transition into active generation. Repeated busy polls do not emit it.

session.idle.description: Material transition out of active generation. Provider event only; not terminal evidence by itself.

session.completed.description: Session reached a terminal completed state.

assistant.message.created.description: Assistant message structure created or role set.

reasoning.delta.description: Incremental reasoning byte or digest change.

text.delta.description: Incremental text byte or digest change.

tool.progress.description: Tool produced a new state, byte count, or digest. Repeated identical progress payloads do not emit it.

artifact.updated.description: Artifact content updated or new version.

permission.blocked.description: A new permission request appeared. It counts once and then triggers the configured noninteractive policy; waiting heartbeats do not count.

question.blocked.description: A new provider question request appeared. It counts once and becomes a blocked protocol condition; waiting heartbeats do not count.

session.status_unchanged.description: Periodic poll showing no state change.

event_mapping.deduplication_key: evt_id within one server_generation. Gateway keeps historically seen evt_ids for that generation, but never infers ordering or completeness from opaque IDs.
event_mapping.progress_clock_rule: The no-observable-progress clock runs only while the provider turn is active. Bounded reconciliation and cancellation have their own retry and deadline policies. Heartbeats, identical polls, identical deltas, and process-alive checks never reset the provider progress clock.

source_observation_and_deduplication.source_observation_key: SHA-256 over canonical adapter_version, server_generation, session_id, and evt_id for SSE events. For REST reconciliation facts, use canonical stable message/part IDs, state, size, and digest.
source_observation_and_deduplication.cursor_semantics: Gateway maintains a monotonic sequence number per session. On reconnect, gateway does NOT send Last-Event-Id; SSE is non-replayable. Instead, gateway runs full REST reconciliation. After reconciliation, new SSE events are appended with the next sequence number.
deduplication_rules: Within a single SSE connection, any event with evt_id already seen is discarded.
No ordering or gap detection by evt_id is used; evt_id is opaque.
Raw deltas must not be stored in the runtime journal; only normalized metadata (type, byte count, digest, locator) is persisted.

reconciliation_mapping.description: After SSE disconnect or ambiguous state, gateway combines multiple independent REST signals to determine session state. No single signal is trusted in isolation. SSE evt_id gaps are not used.

signals.trust: Durable session record. Indicates if session exists.
Presence indicates activity; absence may mean idle, completed, or cancelled. Not terminal on its own. Never sufficient evidence alone.
signals.use: Combined with message state and runtime tree status.

signals.trust: Durable and authoritative for message content, parts, and finish reasons.
signals.use: Primary source for output collection and terminal state (completed, failed, cancelled, error).
Precondition for any reconciliation; if server dead, reconciliation deferred.

signals.trust: Authoritative for whether the dedicated server process is still running.
signals.use: Required for cancellation terminal condition and for detecting server crashes.

reconciliation_rules: If session absent from status map AND messages show a terminal finish_reason (stop, error, abort) AND runtime has accepted the effect (e.g., collected output) -> state is terminal (completed/failed/cancelled).
If session absent from status map AND messages DO NOT show a terminal finish_reason -> state is unknown; do not assume terminal. Mark blocked and escalate.
If session present in status map AND messages show active assistant message with no finish_reason -> state is active; continue observing.
If session is active and the configured no-observable-progress timeout expires, append CancellationIntent(reason=no_progress_timeout) and run cancellation; do not infer failure.
If server unreachable -> state is unknown; retry with backoff; after max retries, blocked and escalate.
If dedicated server tree terminated but OpenCode signals ambiguous -> wait for message reconciliation; if still unresolved, blocked.
session.idle event alone is not terminal; must be combined with a terminal assistant message/error and runtime effect acceptance.
Absence from /session/status map is never sufficient terminal evidence alone.

provider_completed_candidate: The exact assistant message for the delivered user message has a terminal finish value and no error.
GET /session/:id/message returns a stable complete part set whose identifiers, sizes, states, and digests match the reconciliation receipt.
The session is not active in the status map and a material idle transition or two stable REST reads confirms quiescence.
runtime_succeeded: Provider-completed candidate was recorded as ProviderObservation.
Declared role output was validated against the shared envelope, materialized in runtime artifact storage, and has ArtifactReceipt.
Controller appended accepted EffectAcceptance for the effect_key before the succeeded StateEvent.
runtime_failed: Provider error, session error, dispatch rejection, or output persistence error is first recorded as observation.
Controller deterministically verifies it and appends FailureEvidence with category matching terminal_reason.
Missing finish, stream loss, server death, timeout, message absence, retry exhaustion, and recovery exhaustion are never failure evidence by themselves.
runtime_cancelled: Supervisor proves the owned server tree terminated or never launched.
Controller appends cancellation terminal evidence. OpenCode abort acknowledgement or MessageAbortedError is supporting provider evidence only.
terminal_evidence_mapping.conflict_rule: Any conflict between status, message, event, session, profile, or process facts leaves the run reconciling or blocked. No terminal is inferred.

steps: Health check and server version allowlist verification against persisted value.
Re-verify effective profile using allowlisted projection. Any mismatch (including config drift) -> reject resume.
Reconnect to SSE as a fresh connection (no cursor replay).
Reconcile session state via REST using reconciliation matrix.

For any prompt_operation_key with status intended, request_acknowledged, or ambiguous: query messages. Exact message_id plus content_digest proves delivered. Only a conformance-approved pre-effect response proves negative. Absence or inactivity remains blocked and never authorizes resend.
If the previous turn was interrupted, it can become cancelled or failed only with DEC-026 terminal evidence; otherwise keep it blocked and accept no new prompt.

duplicate_delivery_prevention.rule: Gateway-level deduplication with stable prompt_operation_key. OpenCode's messageID is correlation, NOT idempotent. Sending the same prompt_async twice with same messageID results in duplicate user message and duplicate assistant response.
protocol: Gateway generates a stable prompt_operation_key BEFORE calling prompt_async.
Fenced runtime controller commits operation intent with key, content_digest, and initial status intended, then passes the receipt to Gateway.
Before sending, gateway checks if any operation with this key already exists and is 'delivered' or 'ambiguous'.
If 'ambiguous' -> block; do not resend. Escalate to investigator.
If not present -> proceed to POST prompt_async.
After 204, gateway records request_acknowledged and polls messages. Only exact message_id plus content_digest sets delivered.
If message does not appear within configurable timeout, status remains 'ambiguous' and session enters blocked state; escalate to investigator. Never automatically resend.

cancellation_mapping.description: Full cancellation is determined by runtime cancellation intent and server tree termination. OpenCode abort is optional best-effort and its acknowledgement is not required terminal evidence.

stages.action: Runtime records a durable cancel intent with cancel_intent_id, reason, and timestamp.
Gateway may POST /session/:id/abort (optional best-effort). Records acknowledgement boolean.
After requesting OpenCode abort, runtime waits RunSpec.cancellation_grace; if the tree remains alive, it force-terminates only the owned tree.
stages.postcondition: Graceful exit observed or force-termination requested against verified ownership.

stages.action: Runtime verifies server tree is fully terminated (no live process).
Cancel effect persisted in journal. Session marked cancelled.

cancellation_mapping.timeout: If the server tree does not terminate within RunSpec.cancellation_grace, the run remains blocked and emits typed escalation. RunSpec.no_observable_progress_timeout is a separate configured clock.

effective_profile_mapping.description: Before creating or resuming any session, the gateway must verify that the OpenCode effective profile projection matches the runtime's intent. Whole GET /config is never digested or stored; only an allowlisted projection is used.

match_semantics.model: Exact canonical model ID equality. Any user alias is resolved before RunSpec is committed; the adapter never performs fuzzy or substring matching.
match_semantics.variant: Exact equality, including explicit default normalization defined by the compatibility entry.
match_semantics.directory: Canonical absolute path equality after platform-specific normalization and symlink/junction rejection.
match_semantics.permissions: Exact equality of the normalized ordered rule list and its last-match-wins evaluation over every enabled tool/action pattern.
match_semantics.tools: Exact set equality after compatibility-version normalization.
match_semantics.prompt_and_config: Exact SHA-256 digest equality over RFC8785 canonical projections.

checks: Call GET /agent and confirm the expected agent_id is present and active.
Collect effective configuration and provider facts necessary for the allowlisted projection.
Compute a digest of the canonical projection: agent, model/provider/variant, ordered permissions list (last-match-wins), allowed tools, role prompt digest, steps, directory, adapter config digest.
Compare projection against expected values from runtime intent. Any mismatch -> reject.
Verify server version against explicit allowlist (including conformance status). Unlisted version -> reject.
Detect silent fallback (e.g., missing OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR leading to different agent). Any deviation between requested and effective projection must reject dispatch.

compatibility_policy.compatibility: Compatibility is an explicit tuple of OpenCode executable digest, server health version, OpenAPI digest, adapter version, platform, and full conformance result. The CLI wrapper-reported version is diagnostic only and MUST NOT drive routing or feature decisions; executable digest covers launch behavior. Any unlisted tuple fails before dispatch. A changed tuple requires conformance and a new CompatibilityReceipt, not necessarily a new normative contract when behavior remains conformant.
fail_closed_defaults: If any required endpoint returns unexpected status code or shape -> operation fails.
If SSE stream disconnects and cannot re-establish within timeout -> session marked for reconciliation; no progress assumed.
If effective profile verification fails -> no session creation or resume permitted.
If OpenCode version not in allowlist -> gateway refuses to operate.
If experimental v2 routes are present but conformance scenarios fail -> gateway MUST NOT use them.
If duplicate delivery ambiguity cannot be resolved -> gateway blocks further prompts to that session and escalates to investigator.
If server tree termination times out -> session blocked and escalated.

measured_compatibility_baseline.stable_surface_probe: completion, resume, non-replayable SSE, REST messages/status, duplicate messageID hazard, and abort were measured
measured_compatibility_baseline.experimental_v2_probe: rejected: prompt admission did not execute the agent and session wait returned 503
measured_compatibility_baseline.isolated_session_store_probe: OPENCODE_DB pointed to a new per-run SQLite file; GET /session returned zero sessions, existing authenticated providers remained connected, and an isolated prompt completed with finish=stop
measured_compatibility_baseline.dispatch_status: not enabled by this design artifact; implementation must pass the complete conformance suite and issue CompatibilityReceipt

acceptance_scenarios: SC-001: Health endpoint returns healthy=true and server_version; gateway records version.
SC-002: Health endpoint returns unhealthy -> gateway rejects all session and lifecycle operations.
SC-003: GET /agent returns expected agent matching runtime projection -> gateway allows session creation.
SC-004: GET /agent returns a different agent/model/provider/variant than expected -> gateway rejects session creation.
SC-005: Session created via POST /session with explicit agent/model -> GET /session/:id returns matching profile.
SC-006: Prompt_async returns 204 -> gateway records request_acknowledged, not delivered.
SC-007: Prompt_async sent with new content -> a new user message appears in GET /session/:id/message with matching content_digest.

SC-008: Re-sending prompt_async with same messageID -> OpenCode appends duplicate text and starts another assistant response (observed duplicate hazard); gateway detects content_digest mismatch and marks ambiguity, then blocks.
SC-009: Gateway-level deduplication: two calls with same prompt_operation_key -> second call rejected before reaching OpenCode.

SC-010: SSE connection yields a material transition to session.active plus assistant.message.created, text.delta, and tool.start -> gateway emits one lifecycle progress event and three content/tool progress events; repeated busy status does not emit another session.active event.
SC-011: SSE disconnects mid-generation -> gateway reconnects with fresh SSE connection; runs REST reconciliation using message endpoint to rebuild state.
SC-012: SSE disconnect and reconciliation fills state via messages -> session continues normally from last known state.
SC-013: Reconciliation cannot resolve state because messages show no terminal finish_reason and status map absent -> session marked blocked, escalated.
SC-014: Session completes with finish_reason=stop -> gateway collects output as validated envelope with ArtifactRefs; raw content not stored in journal.

SC-015: With fixture no_observable_progress_timeout=10m, the clock expires with no progress -> runtime appends CancellationIntent(reason=no_progress_timeout) and executes full cancellation; it never appends failed directly.
SC-016: With fixture cancellation_grace=10s, cancel intent is recorded and server tree terminates within grace -> terminal cancellation achieved; cancel effect persisted.
SC-017: Cancel intent recorded, abort POST returns true, but server tree still alive -> not terminal; gateway waits for tree termination; if grace expires, blocked.
SC-018: Session is absent from /session/status map but messages show no terminal finish_reason -> gateway does NOT assume terminal; marks blocked and escalates.

SC-019: OpenCode server restarts while a turn was active -> gateway re-verifies profile and reconciles via REST; it never assumes the generation continued and leaves the turn blocked unless typed terminal evidence exists.
SC-020: Resume after server restart but effective profile changed (e.g., model drift) -> gateway rejects resume.
SC-021: Resume after restart and a prompt_operation_key is ambiguous -> gateway queries messages, finds matching user message, marks delivered, and allows new prompt only after prior turn resolved.
SC-022: Resume after restart and ambiguous prompt_operation_key with no matching user message and no active generation -> gateway blocks; does NOT resend old prompt.
SC-023: Configuration without OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR silently falls back to different agent -> gateway's effective profile verification detects mismatch and rejects dispatch.
SC-024: Experimental /api/v2 session creation returns durable events but does not run agent -> gateway conformance check detects v2 non-operational and refuses to use v2 routes.
SC-025: POST /api/session/:id/wait returns 503 -> gateway's v2 conformance test fails; v1 surface rejects v2 features entirely.
SC-026: GET /session/status omits session after idle -> gateway treats absence as NOT terminal; later reconciliation confirms completed/aborted state via messages and runtime effect acceptance.
SC-027: Multiple permission rules with last-match-wins -> gateway's permissions evaluation logic matches OpenCode's effective permission result.
SC-028: OpenCode server version not in explicit allowlist -> gateway refuses to operate, even if server health is ok.
SC-029: Normalized event buffer stores only type, byte_count, digest, locator for message parts - no raw text or tool content passes to journal.
SC-030: Runtime attempts to send raw telemetry into orchestrator context -> gateway rejects; only bounded typed summaries and artifact references are forwarded.
SC-031: Server basic auth with wrong password -> gateway fails connection and retries with exponential backoff; after max retries, surfaces auth error and blocks.
SC-032: Fixture cancellation_grace expires before verified tree termination -> run remains blocked and escalated; cancellation is not assumed terminal.
SC-033: Duplicate prompt delivery suspected but messages endpoint shows no user message after timeout -> gateway marks ambiguous and blocks further prompts; escalates.
SC-034: Gateway creates session, runtime restarts, gateway resumes and re-verifies; if server version changed to incompatible -> resume rejected.
SC-035: Server startup: gateway starts OpenCode on ephemeral port with generated password; ProcessSupervisorReceipt recorded; health check passes; version in allowlist.
SC-036: Server shutdown: gateway sends graceful termination; if process survives, uses force kill; tree termination confirmed; shutdown receipt recorded.
SC-037: Controller crash after create_session: on resume, session exists and profile re-verified; session viable.

SC-038: Controller crashes after prompt_async request acknowledgement: recovery requires exact message_id plus content_digest to mark delivered; otherwise it remains ambiguous and is never resent automatically.

SC-039: Controller crashes while the same supervised OpenCode server keeps running -> replacement controller is fenced, reconnects SSE, reconciles REST, and may continue observing the active turn without resending it.
SC-040: Controller crash after abort requested: on resume, cancel intent present; server tree verified terminated -> terminal; else blocked.
SC-041: SSE disconnect mid-generation: REST reconciliation recovers state from message parts; no progress loss beyond missing SSE window.
SC-042: Active session has only unchanged polls/process-alive heartbeats until configured no_observable_progress_timeout -> full cancellation starts; no failure or clock reset is inferred.
SC-043: OpenCode message shows MessageAbortedError but server tree alive -> cancellation not terminal; tree must be killed; if not, blocked.

SC-044: With a durable CancellationIntent present, runtime force-terminates the verified owned server tree after grace -> termination receipt recorded and cancellation may reach terminal; without the intent, kill alone is not terminal evidence.

SC-045: An unexpected non-system session appears on the dedicated server -> gateway never assumes ownership or ignores it; it records a gateway policy violation, starts runtime cancellation, and preserves evidence.

SC-046: Turn completed -> collect_output validates the shared-protocol envelope, copies declared output bytes into the runtime artifact store, verifies digest, and appends ArtifactReceipt; deleting OpenCode local session data does not lose the promoted output.

SC-047: Controller crashes after POST /session but before receipt -> recovery finds exactly one session by run_id, session_operation_key, and config_digest metadata and records its receipt without creating another.
SC-048: Ambiguous session creation recovery finds zero or multiple metadata matches -> run remains blocked and no second POST /session is issued.
SC-049: Session create response has requested agent/model but GET /session/:id or metadata differs -> dispatch is rejected before prompt submission.
SC-050: SSE emits repeated busy status, unchanged delta, unchanged diff, and process-alive checks -> none reset the no-observable-progress clock.
SC-051: SSE emits a material transition to active or idle -> exactly one normalized progress event is appended; repeated state does not append another.
SC-052: Controller restarts while the same supervised server generation remains active -> fenced replacement reconciles and continues observation without resubmitting the turn.
SC-053: OpenCode server generation changes during an active turn -> gateway never claims continuation; without typed terminal evidence the run remains blocked.
SC-054: Binary digest, server health version, OpenAPI digest, adapter version, or platform differs from CompatibilityReceipt -> dispatch rejects before session creation.

SC-055: Clear server password appears in compiled context, journal, artifact, or log capture -> conformance fails. Same-run child environment inheritance remains reported as a security-level limitation.
SC-056: A user message has the expected message_id but its content_digest differs after duplicate concatenation -> delivery is ambiguous, not delivered, and no automatic resend occurs.

SC-057: Server starts with a fresh per-run OPENCODE_DB -> GET /session returns zero sessions while the explicitly selected existing authenticated provider remains available; no credential value or auth file is copied.
SC-058: OPENCODE_DB is ignored, shared, prepopulated, or unavailable for the pinned tuple -> dispatch fails before session creation and never falls back to OpenCode's shared session database.

SC-059: Two concurrent runs use distinct OPENCODE_DB files and dedicated servers -> each lists only its own session and cannot address the other's session through its server API under conforming behavior.
SC-060: Controller restarts -> it reuses the exact persisted per-run DB locator and supervisor generation; it never opens the default shared OpenCode DB as a recovery fallback.
SC-061: Gateway receives spawn/create/prompt/abort/materialize request without a committed intent receipt from the active controller_generation -> it rejects before external effect.
SC-062: Per-run OPENCODE_DB is new -> authenticated GET /session returns an empty full session list; a non-empty list rejects dispatch even when /session/status is empty.
SC-063: resume_session sees healthy server but current CompatibilityReceipt or exact effective profile is missing/stale -> resume rejects before SSE or prompt operations.
SC-064: observe_events lacks adapter_version or server_generation from current receipts -> it cannot fabricate SourceObservationKey and rejects observation startup.
SC-065: Gateway returns a receipt proposal -> only the fenced controller may assign run_sequence and append it; direct Gateway access to the SQLite source of truth fails conformance.
evidence_sources: installed OpenCode /doc OpenAPI and local conformance probes dated 2026-07-12
residual_limits: SSE is non-replayable per OpenCode v1; OpenCode does not support event replay from a given cursor. Gateway must rely on REST reconciliation after every disconnect, which introduces a window where precise ordering of events may be lost.

Gateway-level deduplication depends on persistent state; if gateway state is lost before delivery confirmation, duplicate delivery is objectively possible until message reconciliation occurs. The contract does not guarantee exactly-once delivery in the face of gateway state loss.
The abort endpoint's true response does not guarantee immediate cancellation; there is inherent latency between acknowledgement and process termination. Server tree kill may also take time.

Session absence from /session/status map is not a terminal signal by itself; this creates a design tension where gateway may hold a session in blocked state unnecessarily if messages are slow to reflect terminal status.
Privacy boundary (no raw content in journal) depends on gateway correctly extracting only metadata. A bug in extraction could leak content; this is an implementation risk, not a contract flaw.

Per-run OPENCODE_DB and loopback password contain cross-run accidents only for the pinned behavior. A hostile process under the same OS user can still discover runtime files or process environment and manipulate the run; this contract does not protect against that.
Experimental v2 surface may become stable in future OpenCode versions; this contract freezes v1 behavior and requires a new contract version when v2 is validated.

OpenCode configuration sources merge with later overrides; if OpenCode changes the merge semantics, gateway's profile verification projection could pass incorrectly, though version allowlist and conformance checks mitigate this.

Dedicated server and database per run limit cross-run session exposure but do not provide cryptographic isolation; password and DB-path environment variables may be accessible to same-run child processes.
Declared role outputs are materialized as artifacts and thus become durable within the system; this is intentional but means they are stored outside the privacy-restricted journal.
